original_abstract:
We synthesized a cerium-based metal–organic framework (Ce-MOF) featuring 4,4′,4′′-nitrilotribenzoic acid linkers via a hydrothermal approach. The resulting architecture integrates Lewis acidic Ce³⁺ sites with triphenylamine cores, imparting potent Fenton-like catalytic properties. The Ce-MOF demonstrated exceptional oxidation activity across a diverse range of substrates, including olefins, primary and secondary alcohols, and hazardous organic dyes. Specifically, the catalyst achieved a 100% conversion rate for cinnamyl alcohol and 53% for styrene, maintaining high selectivity toward benzaldehyde (100%) and styrene oxide (75%). 

Furthermore, the material was evaluated for the oxidative degradation of aqueous pollutants—including Rhodamine B, Methyl Blue, Congo Red, and Direct Blue—using hydrogen peroxide as a green oxidant. Mechanistic investigations confirmed that the catalytic process is driven by the generation of hydroxyl radicals (•OH). These findings highlight the potential of Ce-MOF as a robust platform for advanced oxidation processes (AOPs) in environmental remediation. Notably, the catalyst exhibited excellent durability, retaining its structural integrity and performance over five consecutive cycles without significant loss of activity.
humanized_abstract:
We herein report on the hydrothermal synthesis of a novel Ce-MOF material: Ce-MOF with 4,4′,4′′-nitrilotribenzoic acid (N3TB) linkers and triphenylamine (TPA) as cluster cores. As Fenton like catalyst, the material showed high performance in catalyzing the oxidation of various organic molecules and dyes. 100% conversion of the alcohol cinnamyl alcohol with 100% selectivity toward the corresponding benzaldehyde and up to 53% conversion of olefin styrene (with a selectivity of 75% toward styrene oxide) were achieved together with up to 53% oxidation of primary alcohol ethanolo with up to 100% selectivity toward ethanoic acid and up to 53% of secondary alcohol cyclohexanole with up to 100% selectivity toward cyclohexanoic acid and up to 77% of organic azo dye reactive black 5 with up to 100% selectivity toward chromophore-less product.

For applications in water treatment, the catalytic behaviour of the material was investigated toward the oxidative degradation of Rhodamine B, Methyl Blue, Congo Red and Direct Blue using hydrogen peroxide as the oxidizing agent. The mechanistic studies confirm that the catalytic activity is dominated by the production of hydroxyl radicals (•OH). Moreover, Ce-MOF acts as an excellent catalyst toward water treatment due to its stability. The catalyst maintains the stability of the structure and preserves its catalytic efficiency for at least five on/off cycles in an aqueous medium without any decrease in activity.
